title: MQTT and the Architecture of Real-Time IoT Sensor Networks
subtitle: How a lightweight publish-subscribe protocol became the backbone of distributed sensor data
description: MQTT's minimal overhead and publish-subscribe model make it the ideal communication protocol for low-power seismic sensors and IoT networks streaming real-time data to ringservers and dashboards.
excerpt: A 2-byte fixed header. Asynchronous delivery. Persistent sessions. MQTT was designed for bandwidth-constrained telemetry — and it is exactly what modern distributed sensor networks need.
hero_caption: A distributed network of IoT sensors communicating seismic events via MQTT to a central broker and visualization dashboard.
hero_aria: Diagram of IoT sensor nodes connected by MQTT broker to a data visualization dashboard
